title: podman + docker-compose: DNS does not resolve other containers
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

When using podman with docker-compose, containers don't resolve each others DNS name.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Use this docker-compose file:

```yaml
services:
  postgres:
    image: postgis/postgis:13-3.1-alpine
    environment:
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: password
  another:
    image: alpine
    command: "sh -c 'apk add netcat-openbsd && nc postgres 5432'"
```

2. Run `docker-compose up postgres`.

3. Run` docker-compose up another` in a separate terminal.

**Describe the results you received:**

    nc: getaddrinfo: Name does not resolve


**Describe the results you expected:**

DNS should resolve and this should work.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

- I'm using rootless containers.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.2.1
API Version:  4.2.1
Go Version:   go1.19
Git Commit:   62b324ddf718411b1d4d0ba8117c632f7f984a38-dirty
Built:        Thu Sep  8 08:52:54 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

    export DOCKER_HOST="unix://$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/podman/podman.sock"

comments.body:
I recently had the same issue, and after some confusion I found the root cause of the problem when I manually tried to attach a container to the network:
```
WARN[0000] aardvark-dns binary not found, container dns will not be enabled
```
The problem was that Arch Linux/Manjaro does not have a forced dependency to install the `aardvark-dns` package if you install podman. Manually executing `sudo pacman -S aardvark-dns` fixed the problem for me.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@Luap99 @mheon @flouthoc Is this a packaging issue or a real bug?  Can we close this issue?
Seemingly a packaging issue, yes. Make sure aardvark-dns is installed, and DNS should work. If there is a similar bug where aardvark *is* installed, please open a fresh issue.

I have the same problem as OP. I checked my logs, no missing aardvark-dns is thrown.

```
aardvark-dns.x86_64                              1.1.0-1.fc36                        @updates               
podman.x86_64                                    4:4.2.1-2.fc36                      @updates               
podman-compose.noarch                            1.0.3-6.fc36                        @updates               
podman-gvproxy.x86_64                            4:4.2.1-2.fc36                      @updates               
podman-plugins.x86_64                            4:4.2.1-2.fc36                      @updates               
```

on Fedora 36 Desktop, bare metal.
As said earlier, please open a fresh issue and complete the full issue template
